target.phase_title: Provider result and validation evidence conventions

start_state.isolation_strategy: Create two disposable worktrees from the same start commit instead of using stash-based rollback.

lanes.method: Paid supervisor implements the full FreshForge P16 phase directly, including issue planning, implementation, verification, PR, and closeout.
lanes.rollback_plan: Delete the disposable worktree and branch after recording token/cost and verification evidence.

usage.attribution_note: Goal total includes P50 setup plus the first direct implementation pass; input/output split was not available.

evidence_to_capture: Codex goal token report for the full direct lane
PR or local diff summary if promoted

lanes.method: Supervisor uses Agent Workbench P47 P16 graph packet to delegate proposal-only worker nodes, then performs all FreshForge mutations, verification, PR, and closeout directly.
lanes.rollback_plan: Delete the disposable worktree and branch after recording token/cost and verification evidence.

usage.attribution_note: Goal total includes P50 setup, direct lane, delegated worker orchestration, delegated implementation, and evidence reporting; lane-local supervisor attribution remains unresolved.

evidence_to_capture: Codex goal token report for delegated supervisor lane
worker token counts from provider usage or local estimate
Agent Workbench token/cost records by graph node
PR or local diff summary if promoted

token_accounting.pricing_note: Replace placeholder supervisor prices with the active paid supervisor model prices before final scoring.

direct_supervisor.design: Typed ProviderEvidence dataclass exported from the public package surface.

iteration_1_comparison.current_recommendation: Favor the delegated mapping design for the next FreshForge P16 promotion candidate, while using the direct typed-record design as a documentation and ergonomics pressure test.
iteration_1_comparison.economics_status: Worker tokens are measured, but paid supervisor-token attribution is still too coarse for a final win/loss conclusion.

iteration_2_worker_diff_review.consensus: Both workers selected mapping and identified the same primary tradeoff: flexibility and low migration risk versus weaker typing and consistency.
iteration_2_worker_diff_review.supervisor_interpretation: Promote the delegated mapping branch as the current FreshForge P16 candidate, then strengthen docs and examples instead of adding a public ProviderEvidence dataclass now.

current_promotion_candidate.design: Opaque provider-owned JSON-compatible evidence mapping with recommended documentation fields.
current_promotion_candidate.verification: Targeted tests, full pytest, Ruff, Sphinx -W, build, twine check, git diff --check, and public-safety scan passed.
current_promotion_candidate.remaining_decision: Maintainer review should decide whether to promote this delegated branch into the actual FreshForge P16 PR or request another P50 iteration.

supervisor_goal_checkpoints.note: Includes P50 setup plus direct lane implementation.
Includes delegated worker orchestration, implementation, and reporting.
Includes comparison, worker review, candidate refinement, draft PR, GitHub comments, and reporting.

token_economics_checkpoint_ledger.reason_not_final: Supervisor totals are cumulative goal tokens without input/output split or lane-local segmentation.
token_economics_checkpoint_ledger.current_conclusion: Delegated workers produced useful design signal at negligible cash cost, but P50 has not yet proven paid-supervisor token savings.

agent_workbench_review_surface.purpose: Review Agent Workbench P50 benchmark evidence while keeping P50 open.

decision_rules: The delegated lane wins only if it reaches equivalent or better FreshForge verification with lower paid supervisor token cost.
The delegated lane loses if supervisor cleanup or verification effort erases paid-token savings.
A partial win is allowed when delegated worker outputs are useful but the current protocol needs adjustment.
No conclusion is allowed until both lanes start from the same commit and record actual token usage.
Release, tag, publish, PR merge, and issue closeout remain supervisor-owned in both lanes.
